response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior generalist with ~15 years of experience, solid engineering fundamentals, and some high-throughput distributed systems exposure, but the profile does not match the core of this Principal IC6 role: there is no experimentation, A/B testing, feature-flagging, telemetry, or growth-measurement platform experience anywhere in the resume, and no evidence of principal-level scope such as architecting systems used across multiple teams at enterprise scale. Most roles are senior IC positions at small companies or startups, which falls short of the multi-workstream technical leadership this position requires.
strengths: High-scale, low-latency systems experience (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus authored testing frameworks and taught testing courses
Broad polyglot full-stack background (TypeScript/Node, C/C++, Java, DLang) with recent AI-agent tooling and MCP work
concerns: No experimentation, metrics, telemetry, or feature-flagging platform experience — the central domain of the role
No demonstrated principal-level leadership of multi-team, cross-product initiatives at enterprise scale
Limited evidence of statistical/data-analysis depth (experiment design, bias detection, guardrail metrics)

key_factors.factor: Strong distributed systems and engineering fundamentals
